creator: tatsuki_makino@hotmail.com
text:
I was a little curious and tried a follow-up test, but could not reproduce comment #69 through #71.
Perhaps they are wrong. Sorry.

And the workaround for this problem is to add the -u option to pkg_replace.
I just finished updating with pkg_replace -B -u -a, and in this case there is no problem starting audacious, probably because /usr/local/lib/compat/pkg is kept empty.

The next issue will describe the results of a more precise search for the cause of the problem by reproducing it :)
